title: Vertex Acquires Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for $10 Billion
headline: Vertex announces a $10B cash acquisition of commercial endocrinology biotech Crinetics at ~$85/share (~$8.8B net of cash) — its largest deal ever.
key_facts: Deal value: $10 billion total; $85 per share in cash; ~$8.8 billion net of cash; Crinetics is commercial-stage endocrinology (acromegaly, neuroendocrine tumors, Cushing's pipeline); announced July 6, 2026; Vertex's largest acquisition ever; diversification beyond cystic fibrosis and pain

title: HSBC Downgrades Pfizer to Hold — Seagen ADC Bet Under Scrutiny
headline: HSBC's Rajesh Kumar cuts Pfizer from Buy to Hold, lowering the price target from $32 to $28 after the Phase 3 SigVie-002 failure of sigvotatug vedotin.
key_facts: Price target cut $32 to $28; PFE trading ~$23.67-$23.80, down ~2.12%, ~5% above 52-week low of $23.11; dividend yield ~7.1%; probability-to-market for sigvotatug vedotin lowered to 40%; beta raised 0.78 to 0.85; SigVie-002 (Be6A Lung-01) Phase 3 failed to show statistically significant OS improvement vs docetaxel in previously treated nonsquamous NSCLC (June 22, 2026); first pivotal readout from Pfizer's $43 billion Seagen acquisition

title: Novartis Acquires ADC Developer Myricx Bio for Up to $1.5B
headline: Novartis commits $1.1 billion upfront to acquire Myricx Bio and its next-generation antibody-drug conjugate platform, in a deal worth up to $1.5 billion.
key_facts: $1.1 billion upfront; total deal value up to $1.5 billion with milestones; Myricx Bio is a next-gen ADC platform company; announced ~12 hours before data capture; reinforces the ADC arms race; lands the same day as Pfizer's Seagen-derived ADC trial failure

title: Bristol Myers Krazati Confirmatory Study Fails in Colorectal Cancer
headline: Bristol Myers details the failure of its confirmatory study for Krazati (adagrasib) in colorectal cancer, as Roche's divarasib succeeds in a Phase 3 head-to-head.
key_facts: Krazati (adagrasib) is Bristol Myers' KRAS G12C inhibitor acquired via Mirati Therapeutics; confirmatory study in colorectal cancer failed; Roche's divarasib succeeded in a Phase 3 head-to-head lung cancer study versus treatments sold by Amgen and Bristol Myers

title: FDA First-Half 2026 Approval Pace — Best in Three Years
headline: FDA has approved 23 novel drugs in H1 2026, the best first-half pace in three years despite ongoing staff cuts.
key_facts: 23 novel drug approvals in first half of 2026; best H1 pace in three years; approvals outpacing impact of FDA staff reductions

title: New Acting Deputy CDER Chief — GLP-1 Reviewer Elevated
headline: FDA promotes a longtime GLP-1 reviewer to acting deputy CDER director, following the departure of CBER's top gene and cell therapy regulator.
key_facts: Name not captured; described as a longtime GLP-1 reviewer; follows departure of CBER's top gene/cell therapy regulator Vijay Kumar; acting CBER Director Karim Mikhail now oversees that review office as well

title: Vertex Gains FDA Expansion of Casgevy to Ages 2+
headline: Vertex secures FDA label expansion for Casgevy, the CRISPR-based sickle cell gene therapy, from ages 12+ down to ages 2 and older.
key_facts: Previous approval was ages 12 and older; now expanded to ages 2+; Casgevy is exagamglogene autotemcel, a CRISPR-based gene therapy for sickle cell disease; published July 6, 2026, 11:30am

title: Roche Licenses Breast Cancer Asset from Astex for $25M Upfront
headline: Roche strikes a $25M upfront licensing deal with Astex for a breast cancer asset — small money up front, but a signal for its breast cancer goals.
key_facts: $25M upfront licensing deal for breast cancer; described as small money up front but a big signal for breast cancer goals

title: Scribe Therapeutics Files for IPO
headline: Gene editing developer Scribe Therapeutics plots an IPO, filed roughly four days ago — a promised callback from continuity notes.
key_facts: Gene editing developer; IPO filed ~4 days before data capture; not yet priced; flagged as a promised callback in continuity notes

title: Oruka Drops After Fairmount Unloads $300M Secondary
headline: Oruka Therapeutics shares fell after-hours after Fairmount unloaded 3.55 million shares in a $300 million secondary sale.
key_facts: 3.55 million shares sold; $300 million secondary sale by Fairmount; shares dropped after-hours; early July 2026

title: CMS Proposes 340B Cuts
headline: CMS proposes cuts to the 340B drug pricing program; no dollar details captured.
key_facts: CMS proposes 340B cuts; no dollar details captured in available text

title: HHS to Revoke Remaining COVID Emergency Authorizations
headline: HHS moves to revoke the remaining COVID emergency use authorizations, per the FiercePharma regulatory tracker.
key_facts: HHS to revoke remaining COVID emergency authorizations; FiercePharma regulatory tracker, July 6
